name: Back4app Backend-as-a-Service API
what_it_does: Parse-based Backend-as-a-Service with REST and GraphQL APIs — enabling mobile and web developers to build scalable backends with real-time database, user authentication, file storage, push notifications, and cloud functions, using the open-source Parse platform with managed hosting and a generous free tier.
use_cases: Building mobile app backends via Back4app Parse REST API for iOS/Android data storage and auth
Querying real-time data via Back4app GraphQL API for React Native and Flutter applications
Managing user authentication and ACL-based permissions via Back4app API for app security
Triggering Push Notifications via Back4app API for mobile engagement automation
Running Cloud Code functions via Back4app API for serverless backend logic
not_for: Teams needing SQL relational databases (Back4app uses Parse's NoSQL/MongoDB model)
Complex enterprise workloads requiring advanced query optimization
Applications requiring real-time WebSocket connections without Parse SDK
best_when: A mobile developer or startup needs a managed Parse Server with authentication, real-time database, push notifications, and file storage — with a generous free tier and no infrastructure management.
avoid_when: You need complex SQL queries, have high-volume real-time requirements beyond Parse's capabilities, or need vendor-agnostic infrastructure.

auth.notes: Parse application ID + REST API key (or master key for admin operations). Master key provides unrestricted access — should never be used client-side. REST API key for client app operations. JavaScript key for browser apps. Class-level permissions (CLP) and ACLs for data access control. Session tokens for user authentication (standard Parse auth flow). No OAuth2. No fine-grained API scopes — permission model is Parse's CLP/ACL system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 250 MB database, 1 GB file storage, 10K API calls/day, 5 Cloud Code requests/second
pricing.notes: Brazilian company (São Paulo). Founded 2015 after Facebook shut down Parse platform (2017 shutdown announcement). Back4app became the largest managed Parse Server hosting provider. Open source Parse Server (MIT license). 50K+ developers. SDKs for iOS, Android, JavaScript, Flutter, React Native, Unity, PHP, Python, C#. GraphQL API added in 2020. Cloud Container service for Docker deployment. Container as a Service offering alongside Parse BaaS. Strong in mobile development community.

performance.rate_limits: 10K API calls/day free; 100/second on paid tiers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + GraphQL APIs. Parse API key auth (App ID + REST API Key + optional master key). Good Parse documentation (massive community). Multiple SDK options. Webhook for Parse Triggers (afterSave, afterDelete). GDPR compliant. Rate limits documented on free tier (10K/day). Master key never in client-side code is a critical security rule.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Parse object reads idempotent by objectId. Queries idempotent by same parameters. Object creation not idempotent — check by unique field first. Cloud Code functions require custom idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Master key provides unrestricted access — NEVER expose client-side; use in server-only agents
Parse CLP (Class-Level Permissions) and ACLs must be configured for data security
Free tier 10K API calls/day is very restrictive for high-frequency agent operations
Parse query syntax is Parse-specific (not SQL) — requires Parse SDK knowledge
Real-time LiveQuery requires WebSocket connection separate from REST API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Parse CLP/ACL permission system for data access control. Master key security: must be server-side only — never expose to clients. Parse Session tokens for user authentication. Class-Level Permissions control read/write per collection. Row-level ACLs for fine-grained data access. Open source Parse Server allows security audit. Brazilian company with GDPR compliance awareness.
